blocks.imageAlt: Court building exterior representing legal proceedings
blocks.heading: Court-Ready Evidence, Every Time
blocks.bodyText: Our experts understand what courts need. Every report addresses the letter of instruction, meets MoJ Practice Directions, and is 100% quality-checked before delivery. We've supported thousands of cases across family, criminal, civil, immigration, employment, and prison law.

blocks.heading: Psychologists or Psychiatrists â€” What Your Case Needs

tabs.label: Psychology Services
tabs.content:
Our psychologists deliver objective, evidence-based psychological assessments for every area of law. All are HCPC-registered, enhanced DBS-checked, and experienced in producing reports that meet court standards and comply with MoJ Practice Directions.

Psychology assessments include:

- Parenting capacity and attachment evaluations
- Whole family assessments and child development evaluations
- Fitness to plead and criminal responsibility assessments
- PTSD and psychological trauma evaluations
- Cognitive and neuropsychological assessments
- Workplace stress and occupational assessments
- Risk assessments (re-offending, harm to others, self-harm)
- Mental capacity evaluations
- Substance misuse and dependency assessments
- Immigration and asylum trauma assessments

tabs.label: Psychiatry Services
tabs.content:
Our psychiatrists are GMC-registered and Section 12 approved. They deliver expert psychiatric opinion, diagnosis, and treatment recommendations based on ICD-10 and DSM-5 criteria. When cases need medical-level assessment of mental health, personality disorder, or capacity, our psychiatrists provide thorough, court-ready reports.

Psychiatric assessments include:

- Mental health diagnosis and capacity assessments
- Fitness to plead and criminal capacity evaluations
- Mental state at time of offence assessments
- Personality disorder and psychotic disorder evaluations
- PTSD and trauma-related conditions
- Substance misuse and dependency evaluations
- Risk assessments (violence, self-harm, neglect)
- Care and contact proceedings assessments
- Pre-sentencing and probation reports
- Parole hearing and IPP assessments
- Treatment recommendations and prognosis

tabs.label: When Do You Need Both?
tabs.content:
Many complex cases benefit from both perspectives. A psychologist assesses behaviour, cognition, family dynamics, and psychological state. A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis, treatment recommendations, and addresses medication-related factors. Your Client Manager will advise on which expert type â€” or both â€” best fits your case.

**Not sure?**
 Use our free tool: 
[**Psychologist or Psychiatrist?**](/tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/)

caseStudies.title: 24-Hour Turnaround for the CPS
caseStudies.description: An urgent court-ordered report was required at short notice. Our efficient handling and expert matching exceeded the CPS's expectations and delivered the report within 24 hours.

caseStudies.title: Complex Logistics in Family Law
caseStudies.description: A specialist family firm needed assessments across multiple parties in a complex domestic violence case with funding uncertainty. We coordinated eight individual assessments and delivered on time.

caseStudies.title: Delivering a Court Report Within Days
caseStudies.description: Fitness to plead assessment issues arose mid-case. We stepped in to provide a replacement psychologist and delivered a court-ready report within days.

faqs.question: How quickly can I receive CVs?
faqs.answer: We typically deliver 2â€“3 CVs and quotes within 24 hours. For urgent cases, we often respond faster. Each CV includes qualifications, relevant experience, and a fee breakdown that meets Legal Aid Agency requirements.

faqs.question: Do your experts work at Legal Aid rates?
faqs.answer: Yes. All our experts work at Legal Aid rates for publicly funded cases. We send full fee breakdowns upfront with no surprises. For private cases, we offer transparent quotes with no hidden costs.

faqs.question: What's the standard turnaround for reports?
faqs.answer: Most reports are delivered in 4â€“6 weeks. We offer fast-track services for urgent deadlines. Complex or multi-party cases may take longer â€” we confirm timelines upfront.

faqs.question: Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist?
faqs.answer: It depends on what the court needs. Psychologists assess behaviour, cognition, attachment, and family dynamics. Psychiatrists diagnose, assess capacity, and recommend treatment. Many cases benefit from both. Use our tool: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? â†â€™ /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

faqs.question: Can you handle complex, multi-party cases?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. This is a core strength for us. Our Client Managers excel at coordinating assessments across multiple parties, locations, and solicitors. We handle all logistics â€” you focus on the case.

faqs.question: How do you ensure report quality?
faqs.answer: Every report is 100% proofread and quality-checked before delivery. We verify all instructions are addressed and recommendations are clear. We also vet all experts before registration, reviewing sample work and verifying credentials.

faqs.question: Can I speak directly to the expert?
faqs.answer: Your Client Manager is your main contact. They're fully briefed on your case and handle all queries and coordination. Where appropriate, they arrange direct contact with the expert.

faqs.question: What areas of law do you cover?
faqs.answer: We cover seven practice areas: Family Law, Criminal Law, PI & Civil, Clinical Negligence, Immigration & Asylum, Employment, and Prison Law. Explore each area using the links above.

blocks.imageAlt: Expert reviewing legal documents for quality assurance
blocks.heading: Rigorous Quality Assurance
blocks.bodyText: Every report is 100% proofread and quality-checked by our Client Management team. We verify that all instructions have been addressed, recommendations are clear, and the report meets court standards. No report leaves us without this check.

blocks.heading: Need an Expert Witness Today?
blocks.bodyText: Whether you need a family law expert, criminal specialist, or civil witness, we match you with the right person and handle everything.
